# Turtleand AI Lab

> A hands-on, 6-module curriculum for building practical LLM tools and agents. Self-paced, free, and open.

## Curriculum

### Beginner doorway
- [AI for Beginners](https://lab.turtleand.com/beginners/): A zero-start doorway into Turtleand AI Lab that explains AI in plain language while keeping human judgment at the center.

### Module 0: Fast Track Setup
- [Access & secrets](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/access-secrets): Configure API keys and secrets securely for model access.
- [Safety baseline](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/safety-baseline): Practical safety for AI infrastructure — firewalls, audits, and access control.
- [Simple AI](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/simple-ai): A beginner doorway for understanding what AI is, what it is not, and where to go next inside Turtleand.

### Module 0: Setup & Safety
- [Agent notifications](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/agent-notifications): Get notified when your agent finishes using hooks and ntfy.
- [Running inference locally](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/running-inference-locally): Easily setup & run an LLM on your own machine.

### Module 1: AI Power User
- [Human-AI collaboration handbook](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/human-ai-collaboration-handbook): A quick-reference guide to principles for human-AI collaboration, from mindset to workflow.
- [Model selection & economics](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/model-selection-economics): When to use Opus vs Sonnet, quota management, and cost-per-output thinking.
- [Multi-tool AI workflows](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/multi-tool-workflows): Running 3+ AI tools in parallel, choosing the right tool for each task.
- [Prompt mastery](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/prompt-mastery): Beyond basics — structured prompting for autonomous systems.
- [Voice & multimodal workflows](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/voice-multimodal-workflows): Voice-to-AI pipelines, TTS output, and audio as a primary instruction method.

### Module 2: AI Integration & Orchestration
- [AI-first lifestyle](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/ai-first-lifestyle): Morning briefings, memory systems, and making AI work while you sleep.
- [Automation pipelines](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/automation-pipelines): Cron-driven AI workflows, multi-stage processing, and overnight analysis.
- [How I built a knowledge-base MCP server for my articles](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/building-mcp-server): I built an MCP server that exposes 51 articles to any AI client. Two tools, zero config, no API keys. Here's how it works.
- [Living institutional memory](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/living-institutional-memory): How organizations can use AI to ensure no knowledge ever degrades and no lesson is ever relearned.
- [From OpenClaw to Hermes](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/persistent-agents): Why persistent-agent experiments graduated into a leaner, human-centered Hermes operating layer.

### Module 3: Builder Foundations
- [Context is not memory](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/context-is-not-memory): Why agents need curated memory, not just bigger context windows.
- [Planning and decomposition as control structures for agents](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/planning-decomposition-control-structures): How plans keep agent work bounded, observable, and adaptable.

### Module 5: Agent Architecture
- [The agent loop as a controlled feedback loop](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/agent-loop-controlled-feedback-loop): A beginner lesson on how useful AI agents observe state, choose bounded actions, inspect results, and keep humans responsible for risky steps.
- [Agentic AI systems learning path](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/agentic-ai-systems-learning-path): A concise learning map for agents: loop, tools, memory, planning, approval, reliability, routing, and coordination.
- [Human Agency Is Not a Button](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/human-agency-is-not-a-button): A beginner lesson on why responsible agent systems need authority boundaries, not just approval prompts.
- [Smart Is Not Reliable](https://lab.turtleand.com/topics/smart-is-not-reliable): Why agent reliability is a system property built from checks, constraints, recovery paths, observability, and human escalation.

## About
Created by Turtleand — software engineer building AI education that's practical, not theoretical.
- Portal: https://turtleand.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/turtleand
